title: Homalium retusum
taxonomy: Homalium retusum is classified as a species of plant in the family Salicaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the willow family. It belongs to the genus Homalium, which is a group of tropical and subtropical plants native to Africa and Asia. The genus Homalium is further classified within the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Homalium retusum is a member of the family Salicaceae due to its characteristics such as its willow-like leaves and flowers. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Homalium retusum.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help treat wounds and infections. Furthermore, Homalium retusum is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health.
